channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Follow NPR's annotated fact check of President Trump's address to Congress
item.description: As President Donald Trump addresses a joint session of Congress tonight, reporters from across NPR's newsroom will fact check and offer context as the speech unfolds.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: Houston Rep. Al Green shouts down Trump during presidential address, may face censure by House for the outburst
item.description: Rep. Al Green said afterward it was worth it to make his point — even if he is punished by House leaders, who later called for the congressman to be censured.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Georgia House advances bill to ease death penalty law for intellectually disabled people
item.description: The Georgia House unanimously passed a bill to lessen the threshold for a person facing the death penalty to be deemed intellectually disabled, making them ineligible for execution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in Kabul airport bombing during Afghanistan pullout is caught and heading to US, Trump says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A suspected senior planner in the suicide bombing at the Kabul airport that killed 13 American service members during the chaotic U.S.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia Senate advances pair of bills to ban gender-transitioning treatment for minors, inmates
item.description: Georgia's Senate passed two bills to ban most gender-transitioning treatment for minors and inmates in state prisons, with some Democrats bucking party lines to back the measures.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rep. Al Green shouts down Trump and may face censure by the House for the outburst
item.description: Rep. Al Green of Texas wasn’t the first lawmaker ever to blurt out a protest during a presidential address to Congress.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump proclaims that 'wokeness is dead,' calls to permanently ban sex changes for minors
item.description: President Donald Trump called to permanently ban sex-change procedures for minors while proclaiming that "wokeness is dead" during his joint address on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump promises to keep up 'swift and unrelenting action' in speech to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump vowed to keep up his campaign of “swift and unrelenting action” in reorienting the nation’s economy, immigration and foreign policy as he delivered an unyielding address Tuesday night to Congress and the nation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press and NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump promises ‘this will be our greatest era’ in joint address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump addressed his administration's efforts to end the war in Ukraine, bolster the economy, and crack down on illegal immigration on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fired federal workers are among the guests invited to Trump's speech to Congress
item.description: The Trumps and lawmakers from across the aisle have invited guests to Tuesday's joint address, representing some of the administration's top priorities. Here's a look at some of the names on the list.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration is set to drop lawsuit pushing Idaho to allow emergency abortions, filing shows
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration signaled Tuesday it will move to let Idaho enforce its strict abortion ban, even in the treatment of pregnant women suffering medical emergencies, a dramatic reversal from the previous administration in a closely watched lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Demonstrators across 50 states look to unify a disparate opposition to Trump and his sweeping agenda
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — As Donald Trump prepared Tuesday to address a joint session of Congress, protest groups gathered at parks, statehouses and other public grounds across the country to assail his presidency as dangerous and un-American.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, CHARLOTTE KRAMON and NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada 'a perfect storm for homicides' in domestic violence cases, lawmakers told
item.description: FBI statistics show Nevada is "a perfect storm for homicides" and the state ranks third for men murdering women, the leader of SafeNest said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Justice Department is reviewing prosecution of Colorado clerk who supported Trump’s election lies
item.description: DENVER (AP) — The Department of Justice is backing a former county election clerk in Colorado who was convicted for her role in allowing supporters of President Donald Trump to access confidential data about the 2020 election, the latest move by the administration to use its power to reward allies w
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and CHRISTINA CASSIDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats seek to probe Musk conflicts and DOGE firings with resolutions of inquiry in the House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democrats on the House Oversight Committee are introducing a pair of resolutions demanding the Trump administration turn over documents and information about billionaire adviser Elon Musk's potential conflicts of interest and the firings of federal workers, The Associated Press has
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance's comments about European peacekeeping plan for Ukraine draw ire abroad
item.description: Vice President Vance said the best security guarantee for Ukraine is to sign a mineral deal with the United States.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Zelensky calls Trump meeting 'regrettable,' stops short of apology
item.description: In a post online, Zelensky signaled Ukraine is ready to come to the negotiating table to end the three-year-war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the White House guests for Trump's address
item.description: Fifteen individuals will sit with the first lady, with the invited individuals intended to highlight Trump's accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump and Zelenskyy through the years: From a 'perfect' call to pausing US assistance to Ukraine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The first time he spoke to Volodymyr Zelenskyy, President Donald Trump tried to pressure Ukraine's new leader to dig up dirt on Joe Biden ahead of the 2020 election. It was a 2019 phone call that eventually sparked an impeachment. On Monday, Trump ordered a “pause” to U.S.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy — the top issue in the 2024 presidential election — has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says Zelenskyy wants peace, ready to accept minerals deal after WH blowup
item.description: President Donald Trump says Ukrainian leader Volodymyr Zelenskyy has written to him to say he appreciates U.S. support for his country in its war with Russia and is ready to sign a deal that could ensure future American support.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Macron to discuss nuclear deterrence with European allies. A look at France's unique strategy
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron says that he's ready to start discussions on nuclear deterrence with European allies. Macron has hinted that France could help to protect other nations after U.S.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to deliver high-stakes address to joint session of Congress
item.description: The White House tells NewsNation that Trump’s address will focus on "the renewal of the American Dream" and hinge on four main tenets.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: President Trump pauses Ukraine military aid
item.description: The news comes just days after a disastrous meeting between President Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Paul Manafort: Peace is not in Zelenskyy's interest
item.description: Manafort, who advised former Ukrainian President Viktor Yanukovych, said Zelenskyy has political motivations for prolonging the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms McMahon to lead Education Department as Trump pushes to shut it down
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate voted Monday to confirm former wrestling executive Linda McMahon as the nation’s education chief, a role that places her atop a department that President Donald Trump has vilified and vowed to dismantle.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Some Minnesota child cares closed Monday to call for more state funding
item.description: Hundreds of providers, teachers and families were expected to take part in a “Day Without Childcare” to underscore the need for more funding, affordability and accessibility for kids and parents, as well as living wages for staff.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Education Department offers $25K for staffers to quit
item.description: The Education Department sent out an email to staffers on Friday saying if they quit by Monday at midnight, they will receive a $25,000 cash payout.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5
